sections.title: What this section covers (1)
sections.content: **200 commonly misspelt words** – silent letters, double letters, tricky patterns (e.g. ie/ei, -ence/-ance). Includes exam essentials (FCE Listening Part 2, Use of English Part 2 and 3) and common teen mistakes.

sections.title: What this section covers (2)
sections.content:
• **Check** – test your recognition.
• **Practice** – correct sentences with misspellings.

Tip: Learn in small groups. Notice patterns: receive (i before e after c), accommodation (double c, double m), necessary (one c, double s).

See the reference table on the next page.

sections.title: The word list (200 words) – 1–50 (1)
sections.content: These words appear in practice with typical mistakes to correct.

sections.title: The word list (200 words) – 1–50 (1)
sections.content: **1–50:** accommodation, achieve, across, address, aggressive, apparently, appearance, argument, beginning, believe, business, calendar, category, ceiling, conscience, conscious, definitely, desperate, difference, disappoint, discipline, embarrass, existence, experience, February, foreign, friend, government, harass, height, immediately,

sections.title: The word list (200 words) – 1–50 (2)
sections.content: independent, knowledge, license, maintenance, necessary, neighbour, occasion, occurrence, piece, privilege, pronunciation, receive, recommend, restaurant, rhythm, separate, surprise, weird

sections.title: The word list (200 words) – 51–100 (1)
sections.content: **51–100:** environment, equipment, exaggerate, excellent, familiar, February, intelligence, jewellery, laboratory, leisure, library, maintenance, medicine, miniature, neighbour, occurred, opportunity, parallel, parliament, particular, persuade, possession, preferred, privilege, profession, pronunciation, queue, receive, recommend, reference, relevant,

sections.title: The word list (200 words) – 51–100 (2)
sections.content: restaurant, rhythm, schedule, secretary, separate, sincerely, successful, tomorrow, traffic, unnecessary, until, weather, Wednesday, weird, writing, committee, apparent, beneficial, changeable, committed

sections.title: The word list (200 words) – 101–150 (1)
sections.content: **101–150:** because, although, however, therefore, whether, despite, according, themselves, something, beautiful, wonderful, Thursday, furniture, journey, holiday, adventure, experiment, challenging, determination, effective, motivated, perfection, scenery, employment, security, distribution, curiosity, relationship, inquiry, autonomy, probably, especially,

sections.title: The word list (200 words) – 101–150 (2)
sections.content: actually, really, usually, generally, comfortable, responsible, available, possible, perhaps, interesting, convenient, professional, traditional, January, September, mountain, museum, university, cinema

sections.title: The word list (200 words) – 151–200 (1)
sections.content: **151–200:** stadium, theatre, cathedral, said, people, answer, enough, through, thought, bought, brought, difficult, finally, field, weight, either, neither, different, another, succeed, variety, sincerely, accommodate, appreciates, hierarchy, grateful, opportunity, received, successful,

sections.title: The word list (200 words) – 151–200 (2)
sections.content: environment, experience, separate, surprise, occasionally, beginning, embarrassed, existence, immediately, knowledge, piece, pronunciation

questions.question: Spell this word correctly: accomodation
questions.explanation: Accommodation has double c and double m.

questions.question: Spell this word correctly: recieve
questions.explanation: Receive: i before e except after c.

questions.question: Spell this word correctly: definately
questions.explanation: Definitely has -ite- in the middle, from 'finite'.

questions.question: Spell this word correctly: neccessary
questions.explanation: Necessary: one c, double s (one collar, two sleeves).

questions.question: Spell this word correctly: seperate
Which spelling is correct?
questions.explanation: Accommodation has double c and double m.

questions.question: Which spelling is correct?
questions.explanation: Receive: i before e except after c.

questions.question: Which spelling is correct?
questions.explanation: Definitely has -ite- in the middle, from 'finite'.

Wrong: 'We need accomodation for two nights.' Correct the sentence.
answers: We need accommodation for two nights.
questions.explanation: Accommodation has double c and double m.

questions.question: Wrong: 'She managed to acheive her goal.' Correct the sentence.
answers: She managed to achieve her goal.

questions.question: Wrong: 'We walked accross the road.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'Please send your adress.' Correct the sentence.

Wrong: 'She was apparantly very tired.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'His appearence had changed.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'They had an arguement.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Argument: no e after u (argue → argument).

answers: From the beginning, it was clear.

questions.question: Wrong: 'His consience troubled him.' Correct the sentence.

What is the height of the building?

questions.question: Wrong: 'Reply imediately.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'She's very independant.' Correct the sentence.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Such an occurance is rare.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'Would you like a peice of cake?' Correct the sentence.
answers: Would you like a piece of cake?
questions.explanation: Piece: i before e (piece of pie).

questions.question: Wrong: 'It was a great priviledge to meet her.' Correct the sentence.
answers: It was a great privilege to meet her.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Your pronounciation is very good.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'I recieved your letter.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Receive: i before e except after c.

questions.question: Wrong: 'I would recomend the fish.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'We had dinner at a restuarant.' Correct the sentence.
answers: We had dinner at a restaurant.

questions.question: Wrong: 'She has a good sense of rythm.' Correct the sentence.
answers: She has a good sense of rhythm.
questions.explanation: Rhythm: no vowel between th and m (rh-y-th-m).

questions.question: Wrong: 'Keep them seperate.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'It was a complete suprise.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Surprise: double r, -ise (not -ize in this word).

questions.question: Wrong: 'That's a wierd story.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Weird: e before i (exception to the rule).

questions.question: Wrong: 'We need accomodation and the restuarant is full.' Correct the sentence.
answers: We need accommodation and the restaurant is full.

questions.question: Wrong: 'I definately believe it was necessary.' Correct the sentence.
answers: I definitely believe it was necessary.

questions.question: Wrong: 'The parliment voted.' Correct the sentence.
Wrong: 'Nothing particuler.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Particular: -ar not -er. The common phrase is 'nothing in particular'.

questions.explanation: Whether: wh- + ether (like weather but different meaning).
